steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content:
Lines $l$ and $k$ are parallel, and a transversal crosses both.

The angle $a°$ is formed above line $l$, while the angle $64°$ is formed below line $k$.

Both angles are on the same side of the transversal (the right side), making them co-exterior angles (exterior angles on the same side of the transversal).

steps.title: Apply the co-exterior angle theorem
steps.content:
When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, co-exterior angles are supplementary—they sum to $180°$. Therefore:
$$a + 64 = 180$$
Subtracting \$64 from both sides:
$$a = 180 - 64 = 116$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
